headline: Critical patches for Splunk, Cisco, and NGINX collided with the FortiBleed leak of VPN credentials for nearly 74,000 Fortinet firewalls, leaving enterprise edge and identity gear the day's main battleground.

emerging_trends.text: Stolen VPN credentials and freshly exploited authentication flaws keep steering attackers toward the same internet-facing gear, with the FortiBleed dump alone exposing working logins for nearly 74,000 Fortinet firewalls.
emerging_trends.topic_title: FortiBleed Leak Exposes Credentials for 74,000 Fortinet Firewalls

emerging_trends.text: Attackers increasingly poison trusted distribution channels, as the ShapedPlugin and Mastra compromises pushed malware straight to developers and paying customers through official plugin updates and the npm registry.
emerging_trends.topic_title: ShapedPlugin and Mastra Compromises Poison the Software Supply Chain

emerging_trends.text: Third-party OAuth integrations have become the soft underbelly of cloud CRM, with the Klue breach marking the third connected app turned into a Salesforce data-theft conduit.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Klue OAuth Breach Feeds Widening Icarus Salesforce Theft

topics.title: Splunk, Cisco ISE, and NGINX Patches Land Amid Active Exploitation
topics.latest_developments: CISA added Splunk Enterprise missing-authentication flaw CVE-2026-20253 to its known exploited catalog, Cisco patched a critical input-validation bug in Identity Services Engine that hands attackers root, and F5 detailed two critical NGINX Open Source flaws led by use-after-free CVE-2026-42530 at CVSS 9.2.
topics.summary: Splunk Enterprise, Cisco ISE, and NGINX Open Source all sit at the core of enterprise networks, and the flaws let unauthenticated attackers bypass authentication, gain root, or execute code, so administrators should apply the vendor fixes now. Atlassian and Splunk also shipped fixes for dozens of additional dependency and AI Toolkit bugs the same day.

sources.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
Critical Command Execution Vulnerability Patched in Cisco ISE
F5 Patches Two Critical NGINX Open Source Flaws Enabling Remote Code Execution
Atlassian, Splunk Patch Critical Vulnerabilities

topics.title: Surveillance Exports and Biometric Age Checks Draw Scrutiny
topics.latest_developments: Human Rights Watch found Bulgaria licensed spyware firm Circles to sell to repressive regimes from 2018 through 2023, the UK Home Office advanced facial age estimation for asylum seekers despite its own tests flagging life-altering errors, and Google said it will use UK and EU IP addresses for ad personalization starting August 3, 2026.
topics.summary: The cases span spyware export controls, biometric surveillance of vulnerable migrants, and online ad tracking, each testing how far governments and platforms can push surveillance against privacy safeguards.

sources.title: Bulgaria allowed surveillance tech firm to sell products to repressive regimes, report says
The UK Will Scan Asylum-Seekers' Faces for Age Checks—Despite Knowing the Tech Is Flawed
Google to use UK and EU user IP addresses for ad personalization

topics.title: FortiBleed Leak Exposes Credentials for 74,000 Fortinet Firewalls
topics.latest_developments: BleepingComputer and Help Net Security put the haul at 73,932 firewall URLs worldwide and traced it to a Russian-speaking group that accidentally exposed the stolen configuration files on its own server, where researcher Volodymyr "Bob" Diachenko found them.
topics.summary: The leaked configuration files hand attackers working VPN and firewall credentials at organizations across the globe. Affected operators should rotate every credential and force password resets immediately.

sources.title: FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices.
74,000 Fortinet firewall credentials exposed in FortiBleed data leak

topics.title: ShapedPlugin and Mastra Compromises Poison the Software Supply Chain
topics.latest_developments: Attackers hijacked ShapedPlugin's official WordPress update system to push infected plugin releases to paying customers, while Microsoft detailed a poisoned Mastra npm package whose postinstall payload reached more than 140 projects.
topics.summary: Both attacks subvert trusted distribution—WordPress plugin updates and the npm registry—to plant malware in downstream projects. Teams should audit recent ShapedPlugin and Mastra installs and pin dependency versions.

sources.title: ShapedPlugin update flow hacked to infect WordPress sites
From package to postinstall payload: Inside the Mastra npm supply chain compromise

topics.title: Klue OAuth Breach Feeds Widening Icarus Salesforce Theft
topics.latest_developments: Klue's Battlecards became the third integrated application abused to steal Salesforce CRM data, and the victim roster now includes security vendor Huntress.
topics.summary: The Icarus threat actors run an extortion campaign that pivots through compromised Salesforce-connected apps to siphon CRM data. Companies using Battlecards should revoke OAuth tokens and review Salesforce access logs.

sources.title: Salesforce Data Thefts Continue via Klue App Compromise
Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks

topics.title: Popa Botnet Tied to Israeli Proxy Firm Alarum
topics.latest_developments: Researchers from multiple firms linked the four-year-old Popa Android botnet—millions of consumer TV boxes relaying traffic for ad fraud, account takeovers, and mass scraping—to NetNut, the residential-proxy arm of NASDAQ-listed Alarum Technologies.
topics.summary: Popa conscripts Android-based TV boxes into a residential proxy network that Alarum sells commercially, letting paying customers route abusive traffic through unwitting consumers' homes. The tie raises hard questions for a publicly traded company.

sources.title: 'Popa' Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm

games.recap: Ryan O'Hearn knocks in career-high 6 runs as Pirates roll to 12-4 victory over Athletics

business_politics.title: Bank of England Holds Rate at 3.75%
business_politics.latest_developments: The Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee left its benchmark rate at 3.75% Thursday and trimmed its inflation forecast, though it warned it may yet raise rates if reopening the Strait of Hormuz revives energy-driven price pressure.
business_politics.summary: The Bank of England kept its key rate unchanged, crediting the US-Iran deal that pushed oil lower and eased British inflation risk; the pound fell after the bank cut its inflation outlook.

sources.title: Bank of England holds interest rates at 3.75%
BOE Leaves Rates Unchanged, Signals Caution on Hormuz Opening

business_politics.title: Iran to Receive $6 Billion in Frozen Funds
business_politics.latest_developments: The Financial Times reported the interim accord releases $6 billion of Iranian money held in Qatar, paid out in phases to buy American goods, the first concrete figure attached to Trump's pledge to unfreeze Tehran's assets.
business_politics.summary: Under the US-Iran deal signed at the G7, Washington will free $6 billion of Iranian funds held in Qatar in stages for purchases of US goods while easing banking and transport sanctions; Trump acknowledged Tehran will keep its ballistic missiles.

sources.title: Iran to get access to $6bn of frozen funds to buy US goods
US and Iran sign deal as Trump vows to release frozen funds and ease sanctions

business.title: Eos Energy Opens Second Marshall Township Plant
business.latest_developments: Eos Energy Enterprises began production Thursday at its second manufacturing facility in Marshall Township, delivering on a commitment it made when it announced its headquarters move to the Pittsburgh area.
business.summary: Eos Energy Enterprises started output at a second plant in Marshall Township, north of Pittsburgh, expanding its battery-manufacturing footprint in Allegheny County.

sources.title: Eos Energy starts production at second manufacturing facility in Marshall Township

business.title: 610 Smithfield Building Sells Downtown
business.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette reported that 610 Smithfield Street, a historic downtown Pittsburgh building, has a new owner who may convert it to housing.
business.summary: A historic building at 610 Smithfield Street downtown changed hands, with the new owner weighing a residential conversion in a deal involving broker Herky Pollock and Legacy Realty.

sources.title: Historic Downtown building gets a new owner — and maybe new housing

business.title: Washington County Tops State in Gas Impact Fees
business.latest_developments: Washington County will collect nearly twice as much as last year from Pennsylvania's natural-gas impact fee and again leads all counties, WPXI reported.
business.summary: Washington County will receive the largest county share of Pennsylvania's impact fee on natural-gas production, almost double its prior haul, a measure of heavy Marcellus Shale drilling in the state's southwestern corner.

sources.title: Washington County leads Pennsylvania in natural gas impact fee collection

around_town.title: Pennsylvania Weighs Tighter Vaccine Exemptions
around_town.latest_developments: With measles cases spiking, Pennsylvania lawmakers are moving to narrow the exemptions that let families skip required school vaccinations, the Post-Gazette reported.
around_town.summary: Legislators in Harrisburg are pushing to tighten the exemptions Pennsylvania parents can claim to avoid mandatory school immunizations as measles cases rise across the state.

sources.title: As measles cases spike, Pennsylvania lawmakers push to tighten school vaccine exemptions

around_town.title: Springdale Man Freed After Four Months in ICE Custody
around_town.latest_developments: Randy Cordova-Flores, the Springdale resident local police handed to ICE after a February traffic stop, returned home after four months in federal detention.
around_town.summary: Randy Cordova-Flores, a Peru-born Springdale resident whom local police turned over to ICE following a February traffic stop, is back with his children, though he worries for those still held at the facility where he spent four months.

sources.title: Springdale father detained by ICE back home after 4 months
Springdale man released from ICE detention center 4 months after traffic stop arrest

around_town.title: Kiski Area Splits Tax Changes by County
around_town.latest_developments: The Kiski Area School District's 2026-27 budget raises property taxes 3.2% for its Westmoreland County residents and cuts them 5.5% for its Armstrong County residents.
around_town.summary: The Kiski Area School District approved a budget lifting Westmoreland County property taxes by 3.2% while lowering Armstrong County rates by 5.5% for the coming school year.

sources.title: Kiski Area approves Westmoreland County tax hike, Armstrong County decrease

events.title: Western Pennsylvania Juneteenth Festival
events.latest_developments: The four-day Western Pennsylvania Juneteenth and Black Music Celebration opened June 18 and runs through Sunday, June 21, across Point State Park, Market Square, and Liberty Avenue.
events.summary: Stop the Violence Pittsburgh presents the Western Pennsylvania Juneteenth and Black Music Celebration, billed as North America's largest Juneteenth festival, Thursday through Sunday, June 18-21, 2026, from 11 a.m. to 10 p.m. at Point State Park, Market Square, and along Liberty Avenue, marking the 161st anniversary of emancipation.

sources.title: 14+ things to do this weekend, from Juneteenth and Monster Jam to Summer Splash and Bubblesburgh

around_teams.title: Steelers Likely to Pass on Brendan Sorsby
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette reported the Steelers are unlikely to bid for quarterback Brendan Sorsby in the NFL's supplemental draft, citing his gambling history.
around_teams.summary: Post-Gazette beat writers expect Pittsburgh to stay out of the supplemental draft for quarterback Brendan Sorsby, weighing his gambling baggage against an already crowded quarterback room under coach Mike McCarthy.

sources.title: Don't expect the Steelers to make a bid for Brendan Sorsby in the NFL's supplemental draft

around_teams.title: SNR Drive Builds an All-Time Non-Hall-of-Famer Team
around_teams.latest_developments: On the June 17 SNR Drive, Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler assembled an all-time roster of Steelers who never reached the Hall of Fame and broke down running back Jaylen Warren's game.
around_teams.summary: The Steelers' SNR Drive podcast, hosted by Matt Williamson and Wes Uhler, picked an all-time offense and defense of non-Hall-of-Fame Steelers and analyzed back Jaylen Warren.

sources.title: All-Time Non-HOF Team + Breaking Down Jaylen Warren | SNR Drive (June.17) | Pittsburgh Steelers

around_teams.title: How Spears-Jennings Raised His Draft Stock
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette detailed how a 4.32-second 40-yard dash at the combine reshaped the NFL trajectory of Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings.
around_teams.summary: A Post-Gazette feature traced how Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings turned a 4.32-second 40-yard dash into a higher draft selection.

sources.title: How Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings changed his NFL trajectory in 4.32 seconds

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Michael Morton About E-Commerce in the Age of AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Michael Morton on how AI reshapes e-commerce, pushing back on unfalsifiable bear cases and weighing distribution versus referral models, grocery, and autonomous vehicles.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Exclusive: OpenAI Losses Increased Nearly 8X in 2025, With Spending Hitting $34 Billion
reading.summary: Zitron reports OpenAI's losses grew nearly eightfold in 2025 as spending hit $34 billion, arguing the company still has no path to profitability.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: AI Isn't Breaking Work. It's Already Broken.
reading.summary: Responding to an FT interview with Rebecca Hinds of the Work AI Institute, Newport argues AI isn't breaking knowledge work so much as exposing dysfunction that was already there.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
